commit.message:
fix(blender): add asyncio.Lock to serialize concurrent socket calls

Root cause: viewport_broadcast_loop (every 3s) + /api/preview both call
blender.call_tool() concurrently on the same TCP socket. Without a lock,
write/read operations interleave → one caller consumes the other's response
→ the other hangs for 30s (timeout) → returns 1x1 placeholder PNG.

Fix: asyncio.Lock in BlenderSocketAdapter.send_command() ensures only
one coroutine uses the socket at a time. Others queue without blocking
the event loop.

commit.message:
Document sort order controls for heatmap axes in Lens (#5814)

Kibana PR #244696 added user-configurable sort order controls for
heatmap visualization axes in Lens (Stack 9.4, Serverless). The Style
settings docs for both axes had no mention of this new dropdown.

### Changes

- **Style settings > Vertical axis**: Added **Sort order** setting
(Unsorted/Ascending/Descending) with `{applies_to}` tags for
`serverless: ga` and `stack: ga 9.4`
- **Style settings > Horizontal axis**: Same, with note that sort order
is unavailable for time-based axes (disabled in UI for date histogram)
- **Best practices**: Updated "Order categories meaningfully" tip to
reference the Sort order style setting

Option labels and behavior match the Kibana source (`sort_order.tsx`):
`Unsorted` maps to undefined/auto-detection, `Ascending`/`Descending`
auto-detect numeric vs. alphabetical sorting based on column type.

* docs: update README for MCP and A2A support

* fix(mcp): set streamable_http_path='/' to avoid /mcp/mcp double-prefix

FastMCP by default registers its handler at /mcp inside the sub-app.
When mounted at /mcp the full path became /mcp/mcp. Setting
streamable_http_path='/' makes the endpoint accessible at /mcp.

Also set raise_server_exceptions=False in TestA2AAgentCard fixture:
MCP session manager requires asyncio lifespan context (run()) which
is not available in unit tests, causing RuntimeError instead of 500.

* fix(mcp): optional import, /mcp auth guard, _invoke_agent error handling, revert list_jobs rename

- api/app.py: make MCP import optional (try/except ImportError) so API starts
  without mcp[cli] installed; conditional app.mount('/mcp', ...); revert
  list_jobs_endpoint → list_jobs (unnecessary rename)
- api/app.py: add _mcp_auth_guard middleware — returns 401 when API_KEY is set
  and /mcp request has no valid X-API-Key / Authorization header (A2A Agent
  Card at /.well-known/agent.json stays public per A2A spec)
- shared/mcp_server.py: wrap agent.invoke() in try/except — return structured
  error dict {output:'', steps:0, error:str(exc)} instead of unhandled exception
- tests/test_mcp_server.py: add tests for error handling and /mcp auth guard

* fix(review): address PR #25 Copilot review comments

- api/app.py: error branch now stores metadata-only request_payload

- api/app.py: extract _format_created_at() helper (handles PG datetime,
  ISO-strings and None) and use it in _check_and_process + check_duplicate
  instead of raw str(created_at)[:10]
- api/app.py: _mcp_auth_guard now checks _MCP_AVAILABLE before enforcing
  auth, so /mcp returns 404 (not 401) when mcp package is not installed
- shared/mcp_server.py: use logger.exception() in _invoke_agent error
  handler to preserve full traceback in production logs
- README.md: document /mcp auth behaviour (API_KEY-conditional) in

- CHANGELOG.md: fix mixed quotes «API" → «API»

* fix(mcp): add input size guard to MCP tools

Add _MCP_MAX_INPUT_CHARS = 5_000_000 constant (matching REST API's
ProcessRequest.text max_length) and validate chat_input length in
_invoke_agent before dispatching to LLM. Returns structured error dict
if input exceeds limit, preventing excessively expensive calls from
authorized MCP clients with very large payloads.

Also add test coverage for the new guard.

* fix(auth): GITHUB_TOKEN takes priority over OPENAI_API_KEY for github_models backend

When LLM_BACKEND=github_models and OPENAI_API_KEY contains a non-GitHub key

* fix(mcp_server): disable logger.propagate in stdio mode to prevent duplicate log output

In __main__ stdio block, setup_logger() has already added handlers to the
named 'mcp_server' logger but left propagate=True.  When logging.basicConfig()
later installs a root handler, every log record from 'mcp_server' ends up in
both its own handlers AND the root handler — doubling each line.

Fix: set logger.propagate = False before calling basicConfig so the named
logger's records stay in its own handlers while root-logging still serves
third-party dependencies (mcp, uvicorn, etc.).

* fix(mcp): resolve 6 blocking issues — event loop, CORS, registry, job tracking, middleware, proto

1. Event loop blocking: MCP tool functions are now async, using
   asyncio.to_thread() to offload sync agent invocations to the
   thread pool — prevents blocking /health, /metrics during LLM calls.

2. CORS on 401: _mcp_auth_guard now attaches CORS headers to its 401
   response so browser-based MCP clients see the real error instead of
   an opaque CORS failure.

3. Single AGENT_REGISTRY: MCP list_agents() and A2A Agent Card skills
   now derive from AGENT_REGISTRY (api/app.py) instead of hardcoded
   duplicates.

4. MCP validation pipeline: MCP tool calls now go through
   _create_mcp_job() which creates tracked jobs in the database —
   making them visible in /api/v1/jobs, /api/v1/stats, Prometheus.

5. Middleware ordering: Added comprehensive documentation of the
   middleware execution order and the CORS-header requirement for

static analysis tools before committing code changes.

commit.message:
Build offline mode test suite with sync conflict scenarios (#371)

Created comprehensive test suite for offline-first behavior and sync
conflict resolution using Last-Write-Wins strategy:

OfflineModeTest (8 test cases):
- Local writes succeed when completely offline
- Multiple sets logged offline persist to local database
- Sync operations queued when offline
- Offline to online transition triggers sync queue flush
- Data integrity maintained through offline-online-offline cycles
- Intermittent connectivity does not block local writes
- Workout completion persists offline
- Network timeout during sync does not affect local data

SyncConflictTest (8 test cases):
- Last write wins when local modification is newer
- Last write wins when remote modification is newer
- Concurrent edits to different fields merge correctly
- Identical timestamps favor local version
- Sync interruption preserves partial progress
- Resume sync after interruption skips already-synced entities
- Deleted entity locally propagates to cloud on sync
- Conflicting delete and edit resolves to delete

All tests use MockK + kotlinx.coroutines.test (runTest) following
existing repository test patterns. Tests verify offline-first data
persistence, network state transitions, and conflict resolution logic.

Closes #333

commit.message:
fix: switch imageService from cloudflare-binding to passthrough (#72)

* Initial plan

* fix: change imageService from cloudflare-binding to passthrough

The IMAGES Worker binding (env.IMAGES) is not provisioned — Cloudflare
Images is a paid add-on that has not been enabled for this account.
Using 'cloudflare-binding' caused env.IMAGES to be undefined at runtime,
which broke env.ASSETS.fetch() and produced a 'Page not found' fallback
on every request.

'passthrough' skips image optimisation entirely and passes images through
unchanged, which is the correct behaviour for a static site served from
Workers Assets where Cloudflare Images is not provisioned.

Updated the comment above imageService to document:
- why 'passthrough' is used
- what is required before switching to 'cloudflare-binding' in future

No IMAGES binding exists in src/types/env.ts — no change needed there.

commit.message:
locale: translate missing terms for 15 locales, update english-ok allowlist (#8587)

Translates missing ChurchCRM UI terms across 15 locales (1,941 terms)
and updates `english-ok.json` so the POEditor uploader accepts value=key
entries that are intentionally English (brand names, cognates, locale
names).

## Changes

### Small/medium locales translated inline
- **zh-CN** (5), **zh-TW** (6), **pt-br** (4), **pt** (6), **es / es-AR
/ es-CO / es-MX / es-SV** (7 each), **tr** (6), **fr** (20)
- Terms like `BCC`, `N/A`, `name@example.com` left as `""` per
convention
- `Azure`, `Manual`, `Material`, `Kiosk`, `Malayalam - India`, `Filipino
- Philippines`, colour names etc. translated as value=key (intentionally
English)

### Large locales translated via sub-agents
- **TIER-1:** `hi` (43), `ar` (190), `de` (198), `id` (196), `ja` (190),
`ko` (190), `ru` (189), `vi` (191)
- Denomination-appropriate vocabulary applied per locale (e.g. Coptic
for ar, Lutheran for de, Orthodox for ru)
- Plural forms (`zero/one/two/few/many/other`) filled for Arabic and
Russian

### `english-ok.json` updated
Added entries for all newly translated locales so
`poeditor-upload-missing.js` doesn't skip them as "suspect"
identical-to-source terms:
```json
{
  "tr": ["Azure", "Kiosk", "4. Kiosk"],
  "fr": ["Azure", "Confession", "Format", "Minimum", "Options", ...],
  "es":  ["Azure", "Manual", "Material", "Malayalam - India"],
  ...
}
```

## Remaining (~22 locales, ~3,850 terms)
`af`, `sq`, `am`, `cs`, `nl`, `et`, `fil`, `fi`, `el`, `he`, `hu`, `it`,
`ml`, `nb`, `pl`, `ro`, `sv`, `sw`, `ta`, `te`, `th`, `uk` — not yet
translated. `af` and `sq` translation JSON was produced but not applied
before session ended.

commit.message:
docs: fix masternode collateral amounts in CLAUDE.md

Per protocol spec §5.2:
- Free: 0 TIME (was incorrectly implied as 1,000)
- Bronze: 1,000 TIME (was 10,000)
- Silver: 10,000 TIME (was 100,000)
- Gold: 100,000 TIME (was 1,000,000)

Also add sampling weights (Free=1, Bronze=10, Silver=100, Gold=1,000)
which are the actual values used for TimeVote stake weighting.

feat: add Open VSX marketplace configuration (#50)

Add extensionsGallery configuration to product.json pointing to Open VSX
(Eclipse Foundation's open-source extension marketplace). This enables
extension search, browse, and install functionality in the Extensions view.

Open VSX is the standard marketplace for OSS VS Code forks (VS Codium,
Gitpod, etc.) and is fully compatible with VS Code's gallery API.
